stem: The volume of right circular cylinder A is 30 cubic centimeters. What is the volume, in cubic centimeters, of a right circular cylinder with three times the radius and one-third the height of cylinder A?
rationale:
Choice B is correct. The volume of a cylinder is V = πr²h. Tripling r multiplies the volume by 9; multiplying h by 1/3 multiplies the volume by 1/3. The overall factor is 9 × 1/3 = 3. So the new volume is 3 × 30 = 90 cm³.

Choice A keeps the same volume. Choice C multiplies by 9 only. Choice D divides instead of multiplying.
